axes.label: Faith
axes.prompt: What will the line do with the faith of its time and place?

options.label: Embrace it devoutly
options.blurb: The family kneels with its age — the faith becomes the spine of the house, a source of standing, mercy, and zeal alike.

options.label: Wear it lightly
options.blurb: Observe the forms, believe little — faith as a tool of position, neither embraced nor refused.

options.label: Reject it
options.blurb: Turn from the faith of the age — a dangerous freedom where belief is the social order, a quiet liberation where it is not.

axes.label: Ideology
axes.prompt: Where does the line stand on the order of things?

options.label: Uphold the old order
options.blurb: Hierarchy, deference, and continuity — the family is a pillar of the world as it is.

options.label: Push to remake it
options.blurb: The family bets on change — reform, mobility, the rising tide — and on being its authors.

options.label: Overturn it entirely
options.blurb: Not reform but rupture — the family stakes itself on the world burning and being rebuilt to its design.

axes.label: Society
axes.prompt: How will the line carry itself among its neighbors?

options.label: Keep to its own
options.blurb: Clannish and closed — strength drawn inward, trust kept within the blood and the parish.

options.label: Assimilate and climb
options.blurb: Shed the marks of the outsider, learn the manners of the powerful, and walk through the opened door.

axes.label: The New
axes.prompt: How will the line meet the machinery of its age?

options.label: Seize the new tools
options.blurb: Whatever the age invents — press, rail, engine, code — the family is first to wield it.

options.label: Distrust the new
options.blurb: The old ways are the proven ways — the family holds to craft and land while others chase the machine.
